example.en: I want to go out, but it is raining.
example.vi: Tôi muốn đi chơi, nhưng trời đang mưa.

definitions.en: A word used to connect two clauses that express contrasting ideas.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để nối hai mệnh đề có ý nghĩa trái ngược nhau.

exampleSentences: Cái áo này đẹp nhưng đắt.
Tôi muốn đi chơi nhưng bận.

translation.en: I want to go, but it is raining.

sentenceBreakdowns: Cái áo này (this shirt) + đẹp (beautiful) + nhưng (but) + đắt (expensive)
Tôi (I) + muốn (want) + đi chơi (go out) + nhưng (but) + bận (busy)
exampleSentencesTranslated: This shirt is beautiful but expensive.
I want to go out but I'm busy.
notes:
nhưng = but. It connects two ideas that contrast or go against each other.

It links two full clauses, just like English “but.” Put it between them, no special word order change.

It often shows surprise, contrast, or something unexpected. Stronger alternatives: nhưng mà (more spoken), tuy nhiên (more formal, like “however”).
notes_md:
**nhưng** = **but**. It connects two ideas that contrast or go against each other.

It links two full clauses, just like English “but.” Put it between them, no special word order change.


It often shows surprise, contrast, or something unexpected. Stronger alternatives: **nhưng mà** (more spoken), **tuy nhiên** (more formal, like “however”).

content.sentence: Tôi thích ăn, ___ không có thời gian.

translation.en: I like to eat, but I don't have time.
